intro: Restio pillansii, also known as Pillans' restio, is a type of succulent plant belonging to the Restionaceae family, commonly found in the Western Cape province of South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Restio pillansii include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
